title: With Midterms Coming Up, G.O.P. Is Bogged Down in Congress
summary: Republican majorities are at odds on big issues as the Senate resorts to extraordinary measures to pass homeland security funding over a Democratic blockade.

title: As Democrats Outraise Republican Candidates, the G.O.P. Has This $600 Million Edge
summary: Even as the party faces a worrisome political environment and its candidates are outraised by Democratic ones, powerful Republican groups and super PACs have built a colossal advantage.

title: In Virginia Redistricting Win, Democrats Play Hardball to Thwart Trump
summary: “We cannot bring a stick to a knife fight”: Democrats are increasingly open to extreme measures, including gerrymandering, when the stakes are high.

title: A 60-Day Deadline Could Pressure Trump on Ending the Iran War
summary: A decades-old law allows the president to wage war without congressional approval for 60 days, then limits his options for continuing. President Trump may seek to get around it.

title: Pakistani Officials Remain Hopeful About U.S.-Iran Talks
summary: The seizure of two cargo vessels by Iran in the Strait of Hormuz on Wednesday cast doubt on plans for a second round of talks in Pakistan’s capital.

title: A $440,000 Breast Reduction: How Doctors Cashed In on the No Surprises Act and Arbitration
summary: A law meant to end surprise medical billing accidentally created a multibillion-dollar industry that is making doctors richer.

title: Kevin Warsh, Trump’s Fed Pick, Has Tough Task Shedding ‘Sock Puppet’ Label
summary: Kevin M. Warsh, at his confirmation hearing to lead the Federal Reserve, sought to dispel doubts that he would yield to President Trump’s demand for lower rates.

title: Pace of N.I.H. Funding Slows Further in Trump’s Second Year
summary: The agency has approved far fewer new grants than it did in years past. A renewed effort to screen for disfavored terms and a loss of personnel are contributing.

title: A Capitol Police Officer, Bogus Accusations and a Jan. 6 Conspiracy Theory
summary: Shauni Kerkhoff was wrongfully implicated in the notorious Capitol Hill pipe-bomb case. Can she ever fully move on?

title: Anthropic’s New Mythos A.I. Model Sets Off Global Alarms
summary: Mythos has triggered emergency responses from central banks and intelligence agencies globally, as Anthropic decides who has access to the powerful model.

title: Leaked Code for Anthropic’s Claude Code Tests Copyright Challenges in A.I. Era
summary: Artificial intelligence tools are making it faster than ever to reproduce creative work. Does copyright even matter anymore?

title: Musk’s SpaceX Goals Shift Ahead of Its I.P.O.
summary: As SpaceX prepares to go public, Mr. Musk has proposed moonshots that differ from the company’s original aim of reaching Mars.

title: Trump Administration Nears Loan Deal to Rescue Spirit Airlines
summary: The Trump administration is negotiating a deal with the struggling company, which is in its second bankruptcy in two years.

title: College Where Charlie Kirk Was Killed Revokes Graduation Speaker’s Invite
summary: Utah Valley University was thrilled that Sharon McMahon, a best-selling author, would speak at its graduation. And then her old posts resurfaced.

title: ‘Free Births’ Are a New Pregnancy Trend. Critics Warn About Serious Risks.
summary: Promoters of free birthing reject any type of medical intervention during pregnancy or delivery. The movement has been trending on social media, but critics warn it poses serious risks.

title: Supreme Court Rejects Oil Company Argument in Fight Over Great Lakes Pipeline
summary: The justices sided with Michigan officials, who have raised environmental alarms and pushed to decommission an aging section of the pipeline.

title: Supreme Court Finds Soldier Injured in Suicide Bombing Can Sue
summary: An American soldier was injured in a 2016 suicide bombing in Afghanistan. The justices cleared the way for the soldier to proceed with a lawsuit against a military contractor.

title: Aftershocks from ‘The Shadow Papers’
summary: Publication of a trove of confidential Supreme Court memos ignited debates in the legal academy.

title: Aging in Place: How Technology Might Help You Grow Old at Home
summary: The budding field is turning dreams into reality for older adults who are eager to age in place, filling caregiving gaps and easing minds as America ages rapidly.

title: There’s Another Reason Gen Z Can’t Find Work
summary: It’s not just because of A.I. This started 40 years ago.

title: ‘The Rich Don’t Play by the Rules. So Why Should I?’
summary: Why petty theft might be the new political protest.

title: Rep. David Scott, Longtime Georgia Lawmaker, Dies at 80
summary: The former Georgia state lawmaker was an advocate for issues that affected Black communities. He drew attention for his refusal to exit politics amid health problems.

title: Former Employee Sues MrBeast’s Company Over Harassment Claims
summary: The former employee of the YouTube star’s production company said in a federal lawsuit that she had experienced sexual harassment and pregnancy discrimination. Beast Industries denies all of the claims.

title: Coinbase and Gemini Accused of Illegal Gambling in N.Y. Lawsuits
summary: Attorney General Letitia James said the cryptocurrency giants were illegally running gambling platforms in the state after they expanded into prediction markets, which are loosely regulated.

title: California Is Rife With Hospice Fraud. But Whose Fault Is It?
summary: The Trump administration and Democratic state leaders are blaming each other for what everyone agrees is a big problem. The president has targeted California and Gov. Gavin Newsom in particular.
